title: rosa corymbifera var. pendunculis-pilosis
intro: Rosa corymbifera var. pendunculis-pilosis, also known as Rosa rugosa, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its pink or white flowers and edible hips. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin conditions and protect against cellular damage. The health benefits of Rosa corymbifera var. pendunculis-pilosis include its ability to reduce stress, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
